data.title: Algonquin Power started Overweight at Barclays as 'cheap, compelling turnaround story'
data.description:
Algonquin Power & Utilities (AQN) up 1.9% in Wednesday's trading as Barclays initiated coverage of the Canadian utility company with an Overweight rating and a US$7 price target, saying the stock trades at a discount vs. peers despite a materially improved balance sheet, a refocused pure-play regulatory strategy, and a strong management team.

Algonquin's (AQN) lowering of its FY 2027 earnings guidance on its Q4 earnings call was driven largely by previously conservative tax assumptions and temporary gas safety measures, not a deterioration in its underlying regulatory earnings power, Barclays analysts Michael Lonegan and Nicholas Campanella said.

Management has articulated multiple levers to claw back the reset earnings over time, including tax optimization, earned return on equity improvement, cost discipline, and regulatory recovery of gas operational excellence costs, Lonegan and Campanella wrote.

As billing accuracy and customer service improve—particularly at Empire Electric—regulatory confidence should build and enhance ROEs further, according to the analysts, who also see additional potential upside in several areas, including a possible hydro asset monetization, tax optimization or a re-domiciling scenario, and longer-dated potential from data center-driven load growth in southern Missouri once credibility is fully restored.

data.title: Supermicro duo plead not guilty in server smuggling case: report
data.description:
Supermicro (SMCI) co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw and contractor Ting-Wei “Willy” Sun both pleaded not guilty in a New York City courtroom on Wednesday for their alleged involvement in a scheme to illegally ship Nvidia-powered (NVDA) servers to China, according to Bloomberg.

A third man charged in the case, Ruei-Tsang “Steven” Chang, was a sales manager in Supermicro's Taiwan office. He has not yet been apprehended.

U.S. District Judge Edgardo Ramos set a trial date of November 2, according to the report.

Supermicro's shares have declined by about 30% since the charges against the men became public on March 19.

The trio went to great lengths to hide their actions from both U.S.-based server manufacturers and export control authorities, according to the U.S. Department of Justice. The Justice Department said the server maker had no U.S. Commerce Department license to export servers featuring Nvidia GPUs to China.

The defendants allegedly used hair dryers to remove labels and serial numbers ​from the real machines and placed them on dummy machines left behind after the Nvidia-powered machines had been shipped to China. Their efforts yielded around $2.5B in sales for the server maker since 2024, with $510M sold between late April 2025 and mid-May 2025 going to the Southeast Asian company and on to China.

Last week, two U.S. senators urged Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to consider pausing export licenses that allow advanced Nvidia AI chips and related server systems to be shipped to China or via intermediaries in Southeast Asia.

data.title: Airbus forms Skywise unit to consolidate digital aviation services
data.description:
Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) on Wednesday said it created a wholly owned subsidiary, Skywise, combining its Skywise digital platform with Navblue operations software as part of a broader push to expand its presence in aviation services.

The new entity brings together Airbus’ data analytics capabilities with Navblue’s flight operations tools, aiming to provide integrated digital services for airlines operating both Airbus and non-Airbus fleets.

Skywise will focus on connecting flight, technical and ground operations through a unified platform. The move reflects increasing demand from airlines for integrated systems that can manage multiple aspects of operations, from maintenance to flight planning.

The move underscores a wider trend in the aviation sector, where manufacturers and suppliers are investing in software and data-driven services alongside traditional hardware businesses.

Cristina Aguilar, senior vice president of customer services for Airbus’ commercial aircraft division, said the combined business is intended to integrate technical and digital capabilities into a single offering.

Global footprint and workforce

The subsidiary will employ around 750 people and operate across multiple regions, including Canada, France, India, Poland, Singapore, Thailand, the United Kingdom and the United States.

The Skywise platform, which Airbus said connects more than 12,000 aircraft, has been used primarily for aviation data management. With the creation of the new subsidiary, the company is positioning it as a broader digital services provider.

Navblue, an Airbus subsidiary focused on flight operations software, brings additional capabilities in areas such as flight planning and navigation.

The integration comes as aircraft manufacturers increasingly seek to grow recurring services revenue, particularly in digital tools that can improve operational efficiency and reduce costs for airlines.

data.title: Longest bond market drawdown on record hits 68 months
data.description:
The U.S. bond market is mired in its longest losing streak ever recorded, with no end in sight.

Charlie Bilello said the bond market has now been in drawdown for 68 months as of March 2026, “by far the longest in history.” The milestone puts the post-2020 bond bear market in a category all its own.

In a sign of a structural repricing, the Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate Bond Index drawdown started in August 2020 and has persisted much longer than previous fixed-income slumps, which historically burned out within a year or so.

Bonds absorbed the initial blow from the pandemic-induced inflation shock, then weathered the Federal Reserve’s fastest tightening cycle in decades. Now, they face a market that keeps questioning how low rates can really go, as the war in Iran sends energy costs surging.

The question for bond-market investors is whether sticky inflation and a new oil shock keep the post-2020 drawdown alive even longer, as Fed officials assess energy-driven price pressures bleeding into broader inflation and near-term expectations.

data.title: AeroVironment selected for U.S. Navy ISR services contract
data.description:
AeroVironment (AVAV) on Tuesday said it was selected by the U.S. Navy to participate in a contractor-owned, contractor-operated intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance services program aimed at expanding and modernizing naval capabilities.

Under the initiative, AeroVironment (AVAV) will compete with other industry partners for delivery orders to provide ISR services supporting naval operations. The program is structured to deliver persistent surveillance using a mix of autonomous systems, integrated sensors and operational support.

Program structure and scope

The contract vehicle allows the Navy to procure ISR services rather than directly owning and operating all equipment. Companies selected for the program are expected to provide turnkey capabilities, including aircraft, sensors and mission support personnel.

AeroVironment (AVAV) said its offering will include its JUMP 20-X uncrewed aircraft system, a Group 3 vertical take-off and landing platform designed for both land and maritime missions.

Aircraft capabilities

The JUMP 20-X system is designed to operate without traditional launch and recovery equipment, which can reduce logistical requirements in constrained environments. The aircraft has an endurance of more than 13 hours, a range of about 115 miles and can carry payloads of up to 30 pounds (13.61 kg).

The platform supports multiple sensor configurations, allowing it to be adapted for different ISR missions, including maritime surveillance and ground-based reconnaissance.

Prior deployments

AeroVironment (AVAV) has previously provided ISR-related support to several U.S. and allied units, including operations tied to U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command, the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit and the Korean Navy.

The company did not disclose the potential value of the Navy program or the number of competitors selected.

The award reflects the Pentagon’s broader push to expand unmanned and contractor-operated ISR capabilities as part of efforts to increase operational flexibility and reduce the burden on uniformed personnel.

data.title: Phelan Green selects Honeywell tech for green jet fuel facility in South Africa
data.description:
A subsidiary of Phelan Green on Wednesday said it selected fuel-processing technology from Honeywell International (HON) for a planned sustainable aviation fuel facility in South Africa, as developers move forward with one of the country’s largest green energy projects.

Phelan eFuels said it will use Honeywell UOP’s Fischer-Tropsch Unicracking process at a proposed site in Saldanha Bay in the Western Cape. The technology is designed to convert synthetic feedstocks derived from carbon dioxide into electro-sustainable aviation fuel, or eSAF, that meets industry specifications.

The facility is part of the broader Phelan Green Hydrogen Project, a private investment valued at about 47 billion rand, or roughly $2.5 billion. South African authorities have designated the initiative as a strategic industrial development aimed at expanding the country’s role in low-carbon energy production.

Once operational, the project is expected to produce more than 140,000 metric tons of eSAF annually, with output primarily targeted for export to European Union and U.K. markets, where demand for lower-emissions aviation fuel is rising.

Construction of the facility is scheduled to begin in the fourth quarter of 2026.

data.title: Consumer staples win on defensive bets in Q1; Bunge, ADM lead gains
data.description:
U.S. equities closed Q1 on a weak footing, with the benchmark S&P 500 posting a quarterly decline of ~4.6%, while consumer staples emerged as a rare pocket of strength as investors rotated into defensive sectors.

The consumer sector only lagged the top-performing energy sector (XLE) and materials (XLB) in Q1 2026.

The XLP saw an outflow of approximately $6M, which compares to a massive $30B outflow from the broader S&P 500 (SPY) fund. The month of March saw considerable outflows in XLP (XLP).

The Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLP) outperformed the broader market, delivering gains of close to 5.5% as investors sought stability in companies with predictable earnings and pricing power.

Within staples, performance was sharply divided, with upstream food suppliers and large retailers leading gains. Bunge Global (BG) surged more than 40% in the quarter, while Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) advanced over 23%, supported by strong commodity trading and processing margins. Retailers including Target (TGT) and Kroger (KR) rose about 22% and 18%, respectively, benefiting from resilient consumer demand and improved traffic trends.

Top 5 performers (Q1 2026)

  • Bunge Global (BG)  +40.81%
  • Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) +23.35%
  • Target (TGT) +21.91%
  • Kroger (KR) +17.61%
  • Hershey (HSY) +17.21%

Bottom 5 performers (Q1 2026)

  • Estée Lauder (EL) -35.77%
  • McCormick (MKC) -21.55%
  • Campbell Soup (CPB) -20.96%
  • General Mills (GIS) -20.48%
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR) -14.74%

However, several packaged food companies lagged, reflecting margin pressure and softer volumes. General Mills (GIS), Campbell (CPB), and McCormick each fell more than 20% during the quarter. Discount retailers Dollar Tree (DLTR) and Dollar General (DG) also declined sharply, signaling ongoing strain among lower-income consumers. 

The biggest drag came from Estée Lauder (EL), which tumbled nearly 36%, hit by weak international demand and continued pressures in key Asian markets.

SA Analyst comments

"Consumer staples stocks enter 2026 with deeply reset expectations, compressed valuations, and high short interest, creating an asymmetric risk/reward setup." wrote an SA contributor.

"Although the consumer staples sector shares some similarities and is even more resilient when it comes to macroeconomic impacts, I am more bearish on this industry in the short term, and I believe that the current situation is much more complex than it appears to be." said another SA contributor Kenio Fontes.

Other funds for a diversified approach to consumer staples include: Vanguard Consumer Staples ETF (VDC); iShares U.S. Consumer Staples ETF (IYK); First Trust Consumer Staples AlphaDEX Fund (FXG); Invesco Dynamic Food & Beverage ETF (PBJ); First Trust Nasdaq Food & Beverage ETF (FTXG); iShares Global Consumer Staples ETF (KXI).

data.title: ServiceNow's Armis acquisition offers several benefits, Morgan Stanley says
data.description:
• ServiceNow's (NOW) pending deal to acquire Armis could offer several benefits to the Bill McDermott-led enterprise software company once the deal closes, Morgan Stanley said.
  • “Shift in M&A philosophy has been an understandable concern, though Armis provides benefits beyond incremental growth potential... ...including broadening the TAM. [Operational technology] has been an area of focus for ServiceNow and Armis further solidifies its positioning in an increasingly important market,” analyst Keith Weiss wrote in a note to clients. “More importantly, Armis enhances ServiceNow's CMDB with robust physical asset data, enhancing ServiceNow's end-to-end workflow capabilities.”
  • Weiss, who has an Overweight rating and a $210 price target on ServiceNow, said the Armis deal can help ServiceNow in four ways: it closes the physical asset visibility gap; it boosts the physical asset library; it improves existing solutions and security capabilities; and it increases end-to-end workflow.
  • “While ServiceNow has succeeded in enhancing their digital data store through expanding horizontally into departments beyond IT and becoming a de-facto enterprise system-of-record, the company's physical asset library has lagged with OT being an area of focus, though a smaller contributor to the business over the past several years,” Weiss explained. “Armis expands upon the latter opportunity, providing robust physical asset data that ServiceNow can integrate into their CMDB giving enterprises a unified view of digital and physical environments. This unification subsequently enhances ServiceNow's existing solutions and security capabilities with specific workflow offerings such as ITAM, ITSM, ITOM, SecOps, and IRM/GRC well-positioned to benefit.”
